text: The near-infrared mode is activated, revealing a signal in the anterior plane of dissection during the transanal total mesorectal excision.
The surgical field shows the area where the near-infrared signal is visible, with no white light present in this region.
The abdominal team removes their light source from the abdomen while the near-infrared mode remains activated, allowing visualization of the surgical field without standard illumination.
The abdominal light source is reintroduced into the surgical field, revealing a near-infrared light signal on the pinpoint display, highlighting the area of interest in the plane of dissection.
The near-infrared light signal intensifies as the abdominal and transanal teams approach the junction point during the rectal cancer procedure.
